question: Did the model explicitly select Option B as the missing piece to complete the image?
Is the final answer presented in a clearly identifiable form (e.g., exactly one option selected, without ambiguity)?
Does the model correctly identify that the legs visible in the main image (brown loafers on the left, white sneakers on the right) match the upper bodies of the girl and boy in Option B?
Does the model's reasoning cite the continuation of the jet trail/airplane contrail and the sky gradient from the top of the main image into the missing piece?
Does the model identify continuity in background infrastructure (e.g., power lines, building edges, or the road) to justify the fit of Option B?
Did the model correctly identify the semantic content of the missing piece as a scene involving two students exchanging a letter (confession scene)?
Did the model provide valid reasoning for rejecting other options (e.g., Option A is legs only, Option C is upside down, Option D is sky only/sideways)?
If the model generated a reconstructed image, is the white rectangular gap filled with the content from Option B without leaving visible white space or overlaps?
If the model generated intermediate or final images, is the artistic style and resolution consistent with the original image (avoiding hallucinations or obvious style shifts)?
In the final reconstructed image (if generated), does the skyline (clouds, contrail, birds) remain continuous across the top boundary of the inserted piece?
If the model generated a final image, are the characters correctly oriented and positioned (Girl on the left, Boy on the right) relative to the scene context?
If the text describes a specific intermediate action (e.g., 'rotating the piece'), does the corresponding generated image faithfully reflect this action?
Does the model's textual description of spatial relationships (e.g., 'girl is on the left') match the visual evidence in the generated images?
If the model mentions matching specific background elements (e.g., 'YAMATO truck', 'houses'), are these elements actually visible in the option piece being discussed?
Does the model's textual description of the rotation applied to the chosen piece match what is shown in any generated visual demonstration?
Are there any contradictions between the model's textual reasoning and the visual evidence shown in generated images (e.g., claiming alignment while showing a mismatch)?
